evals.prompt: Our Meta video creative has been running 4 weeks. ROAS dropped from 3.1 to 1.8. What's happening?
evals.expected_output: Likely creative fatigue. Walks the fatigue-signals framework (CTR drop, frequency rise, CPM rise as leading; ROAS drop as lagging). Notes 4 weeks is twice Meta's ~2-week fatigue cycle. Recommends new creative as the highest-leverage refresh. References ads for the broader channel strategy and ab-testing for the test design.
assertions: Recommends new creative as highest-leverage refresh
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: We need creative for cold-traffic TikTok. What works?
evals.expected_output: Walks the platform-native discipline for TikTok specifically: vertical, in-app feel, raw-looking, on-screen captions, on-trend audio, hand-held movement, creator face-forward, fast cuts. Walks the hook-body-CTA structure with TikTok-specific examples. Recommends UGC or UGC-style creator content over polished production. Recommends 3-5 creative concepts per week to match TikTok's 3-day fatigue cycle.
assertions: Walks the platform-native discipline for TikTok
Names TikTok-specific cues (vertical, in-app, raw, captions)
Recommends UGC or UGC-style over polished production
Names TikTok's 3-day fatigue cycle and required cadence
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: We tested 5 creatives at once and one won. We're going to scale it. Good?
evals.expected_output: Pushback on the multi-variable test. Walks the one-variable-per-test rule. Notes that 5 creatives varying hook, body, CTA, and format simultaneously do not isolate what won; the next round of testing will repeat the mistake. Recommends a follow-up isolation pass: hold the winning creative's body and CTA constant and test variant hooks. References ab-testing for the methodology.
assertions: Pushes back on the multi-variable test
Notes the next round will repeat the mistake without isolation

evals.prompt: Should we use real UGC or hire UGC-style creators?
evals.expected_output: Both, depending on placement. Walks the real-vs-lookalike-UGC framework. Recommends real UGC for highest-leverage placements (cold Meta, cold TikTok). Recommends lookalike UGC for volume needs. Names criteria: sophisticated audience favors real; visible-transformation products favor real; high-volume need favors lookalike. Notes the AI image-generation tooling lineup (Gemini Nano Banana Pro, Flux Pro 1.1, Ideogram 3.0, ChatGPT Images 2.0, Midjourney v7, Recraft V3, Stable Diffusion 3.5) for static UGC-style assets, with the rule never to claim AI-generated output as real customer content.
assertions: Names the May 2026 image-generation lineup (at least 3 of: Gemini Nano Banana Pro, Flux Pro 1.1, Ideogram 3.0, ChatGPT Images 2.0, Midjourney v7, Recraft V3, Stable Diffusion 3.5)
Warns against claiming AI-generated UGC as real customer content

evals.prompt: Write 3 hooks for a daily greens powder targeting cold Meta audiences.
evals.expected_output: Three hook concepts using different mechanics (specific outcome claim, pattern-interrupt visual, customer testimonial, question, comparison). Each tagged with structure and rationale. References copywriting for the hook-as-promise framing. Walks the first-3-seconds framework.
assertions: Uses different mechanics across the three (claim, visual, testimonial, question, etc.)

evals.prompt: Our static ads on Meta keep getting beaten by video. Should we stop running statics?
evals.expected_output: Likely yes for cold traffic, no for retargeting. Walks the creative-format-selection framework: cold-traffic statics generally underperform video except on Pinterest and Google Display; retargeting statics can work because the visitor already knows the brand. Recommends shifting static to retargeting and producing video for cold. References ads for the channel-mix decision.
assertions: Notes Pinterest and Google Display as static-friendly exceptions
References ads for the channel-mix decision

evals.prompt: Our hooks are weak. Our top creatives all have hooks like 'New Product Launch.' What should we do?
evals.expected_output: Replace the generic hooks. Walks the hook-body-CTA structure with strong hook examples (specific outcome claim, pattern-interrupt visual, customer testimonial, comparison, question). Recommends mining top customer reviews and reframing the strongest customer quotes as hooks. References copywriting for the customer-language-not-company-language framework and customer-research for the JTBD inputs.
assertions: Identifies the hooks as generic and underleveraged
Provides 3-5 stronger hook examples by mechanic
Recommends mining customer reviews for hook source
